subject: [PATCH v2 019/110] autofs: use PRIino format for i_ino

reviews.author: Jeff Layton
reviews.summary: This patch series is a part of a larger effort to widen i_ino via kino_t. The changes are necessary for the upcoming change and should be applied as soon as possible.
reviews.raw_body:
Convert autofs i_ino format strings to use the PRIino format
macro in preparation for the widening of i_ino via kino_t.

Also correct signed format specifiers to unsigned, since inode
numbers are unsigned values.

Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
---
 fs/autofs/inode.c | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/fs/autofs/inode.c b/fs/autofs/inode.c
index c53dc551053ba53fa7c85ca57eb877fff74a4ed1..ef3444d57f6ee15767f744a5ecda020c247095d7 100644
--- a/fs/autofs/inode.c
+++ b/fs/autofs/inode.c
@@ -92,7 +92,7 @@ static int autofs_show_options(struct seq_file *m, struct dentry *root)
 		seq_puts(m, ",ignore");
 #ifdef CONFIG_CHECKPOINT_RESTORE
 	if (sbi->pipe)
-		seq_printf(m, ",pipe_ino=%ld", file_inode(sbi->pipe)->i_ino);
+		seq_printf(m, ",pipe_ino=%" PRIino "u", file_inode(sbi->pipe)->i_ino);
 	else
 		seq_puts(m, ",pipe_ino=-1");
 #endif

-- 
2.53.0


______________________________________________________
Linux MTD discussion mailing list
http://lists.infradead.org/mailman/listinfo/linux-mtd/

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch series converts the autofs module to use PRIino format for i_ino, preparing it for the widening of i_ino via kino_t. It also corrects signed format specifiers to unsigned, as inode numbers are unsigned values.
